system.effect: The Sorcerer places a curse on a target creature or object. A creature may throw INT 9+ to resist the curse. The caster must choose one of the following effects as the curse: Halve one characteristic score (round up); -3 modifier to all attack throws; Panic: each round of combat, the target must throw 1D; on 1-3, it acts normally on 4-6, it takes no action. The caster may invent a different curse but should not be more powerful than the examples above; this is at the Referee's discretion. The curse is permanent, unless removed by the Expunge Curse spell.
system.critSuccess: Any Expunge Curse spell cast to remove the curse is at a -4 modifier, and if it fails, it may not be cast on the same victim until a week passes.
